title: rubus × rhomboidalis
intro: Rubus × rhomboidalis, also known as the thimbleberry hybrid, is a cross between the Rubus ursinus and Rubus parviflorus species, resulting in a unique and adaptable plant. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus × rhomboidalis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
